title: Crack the Vault
module_title: Hash Cracking and Password Attack Methodology
prerequisites: Hashcat and John the Ripper usage
learning_objectives: Accurately identify hash types for proper cracking
Execute wordlist-based attacks with standard dictionaries
Create custom wordlists targeting specific organizations
Apply rule-based attacks for password variations
Analyse password patterns and produce security assessments
scenario: Captured hashes from previous missions. Crack them, build custom wordlists, and produce a password hygiene assessment.

scenario_context.setting: Collection of hashes from multiple penetration tests
scenario_context.challenge: Crack diverse hash types and assess organizational password policies
scenario_context.relevance: Password cracking is critical for breach assessment and security improvement

subtasks.title: Hash Identification
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Analyse hash format, length, character set. Use hash-identifier tool. Create identification table documenting type per hash.

subtasks.title: Standard Wordlist Attack
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use Hashcat or John with rockyou.txt wordlist. Document success rate. Create results table with hash and cracked password.

subtasks.title: Custom Wordlist Creation
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use CeWL to generate custom wordlist from target website. Combine with standard lists. Test against remaining uncracked hashes.

subtasks.title: Rule-Based Attacks
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Apply rule-based attacks using best64.rule and dive.rule. Iterate through remaining uncracked hashes. Document rule effectiveness.

subtasks.title: Password Hygiene Report
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Analyse cracked passwords for patterns: length, complexity, reuse, dictionary words. Create executive assessment with recommendations.

resources.name: Hashcat and John the Ripper Guide

reflection_prompt: What password patterns did you discover? How would you advise the organization to strengthen password policies?

quiz.question: Why is custom wordlist creation valuable in password cracking?
options: Target-specific wordlists increase crack rate and efficiency
quiz.explanation: Custom wordlists tailored to the organization (using CeWL on their website) significantly increase success rates by targeting likely passwords.

quiz.question: What is the primary advantage of rule-based attacks?
options: Generate password variations automatically from base wordlist
quiz.explanation: Rules apply transformations to base passwords (e.g., capitalization, numbers, special chars), covering common password variations efficiently.

mentor_notes: Assess hash identification accuracy and cracking strategy. Custom wordlist quality shows targeting capability. Analysis should drive organizational recommendations. Success rate less important than methodology.
